summary_intro: Patient presents with a complex, multi-decade history of neurodevelopmental symptoms, multiple misdiagnoses, and chronic employment instability. Early Medical and Surgical History includes premature birth at 34 weeks and emergency surgery for a ruptured appendix at age 9.

summary_sections.heading: Current Health and Core Symptoms
summary_sections.content: Chronic gastrointestinal distress and completely inverted sleep schedule, rarely falling asleep before 4:00 AM.

summary_sections.heading: Academic and Behavioral History
summary_sections.content: Struggled significantly in middle school, frequently missing homework assignments and failing to bring textbooks. Placed in remedial math classes despite advanced aptitude for reading and language comprehension. Received a week-long suspension for impulsively pulling a fire alarm on a dare.

summary_sections.heading: Employment History
summary_sections.content: Held approximately 14 different jobs across retail, food service, and tech sectors. Longest position lasted eight months due to boredom or interpersonal conflicts with management.

summary_sections.heading: Social and Relational History
summary_sections.content: Difficulty maintaining long-term friendships; partners express frustration over financial impulsivity and lack of long-term planning. Engaged twice but both relationships ended.

summary_sections.heading: Psychological Testing History
summary_sections.content: Partial neuropsychological evaluation at age 21 suggested a mood disorder, but no definitive diagnosis was reached. Attempted cognitive behavioral therapy at age 24 but dropped out after three sessions due to patronizing homework assignments.

summary_sections.heading: Substance Use History
summary_sections.content: Reports using caffeine excessively, consuming energy drinks throughout the day to self-medicate for chronic brain fog.

details.heading: Early Medical and Surgical History
details.content: Born prematurely at 34 weeks and spent three weeks in NICU. Emergency surgery for a ruptured appendix at age 9, resulting in a month-long hospital stay and significant academic disruption.

details.heading: Academic Performance
details.content: Struggled significantly in middle school, frequently missing homework assignments and failing to bring textbooks. Placed in remedial math classes despite advanced aptitude for reading and language comprehension. Received a week-long suspension for impulsively pulling a fire alarm on a dare.

details.heading: Employment Stability
details.content: Held approximately 14 different jobs across retail, food service, and tech sectors. Longest position lasted eight months due to boredom or interpersonal conflicts with management.

details.heading: Social Interactions
details.content: Difficulty maintaining long-term friendships; partners express frustration over financial impulsivity and lack of long-term planning. Engaged twice but both relationships ended.

details.heading: Psychological Evaluation
details.content: Partial neuropsychological evaluation at age 21 suggested a mood disorder, but no definitive diagnosis was reached. Attempted cognitive behavioral therapy at age 24 but dropped out after three sessions due to patronizing homework assignments.

details.heading: Substance Use
details.content: Reports using caffeine excessively, consuming energy drinks throughout the day to self-medicate for chronic brain fog.

next_steps: Full, multi-hour neuropsychological battery will be scheduled for next month.
Request release forms to speak with previous therapist to obtain records from age 21 neuropsychological evaluation.
Begin tracking caffeine intake and sleep hours in a dedicated journal app before any pharmacological interventions are discussed.
